title: Fix task completion timestamp format
summary: Feedback #41 reports that the task completion note appended to the plan progress section currently uses an ISO timestamp such as 2026-07-07T14:06:06.811Z, but the expected display format is yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss. The affected write path is markTaskCompletedInPlan in src/loop/taskExecution.js. Internal database and event timestamps can keep the existing ISO format; only the Markdown progress note should use the user-facing second-level date-time format.

tasks.title: Format AutoPlan completion notes as yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss
acceptance: The progress-section note written by markTaskCompletedInPlan, for example `- P001 AutoPlan completed: ...`, uses `yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss` and does not contain `T`, milliseconds, or `Z`.
The formatter is applied only to the Markdown progress note; internal fields such as task finishedAt, event metadata, and database updated_at keep their existing ISO timestamp semantics.
Completion notes with and without a relative log path keep the existing note structure, with only the displayed timestamp changed.

tasks.title: Add regression coverage for progress note timestamps
acceptance: First-time completion writeback asserts that the progress-section timestamp matches `\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2} \d{2}:\d{2}:\d{2}`.
Repeated writeback for the same task still keeps only one AutoPlan completion note and the replacement note uses the target timestamp format.
When the completion note includes a relative log path, the log path remains after the timestamp and existing checkbox update plus legacy progress-table cleanup behavior does not regress.

criteria: Static checks pass with no syntax or TypeScript errors.
Task execution tests pass and AutoPlan completion notes in plan progress sections no longer use ISO-shaped timestamps.
The full test suite passes, with no regression in task completion, retry writeback, log path writeback, or plan progress refresh behavior.
